purpose: Narrow phase-01 repair for gradle.properties config over-detections.

set.sourceBehavior: `gradle.properties` only defines Gradle/Kotlin daemon JVM args, AndroidX enablement, KAPT worker disabling, and Android resource/build flags; it does not declare any WorkManager, alarm, job, worker, or background scheduling behavior.
set.targetBehavior: HarmonyOS phase-01 keeps build-time configuration separate from runtime behavior, so no background task or scheduler endpoint is introduced from this config file.
verificationEvidence: manual-review: source `gradle.properties` contains only daemon memory/JDK-open flags, AndroidX, KAPT, and resource/build toggles.
manual-review: target runtime entry remains limited to `EntryAbility.onCreate` startup resolution and does not add scheduler surfaces for this source config.
set.notes: Static analysis over-detected background-work risk on a build tuning file. The source config does not express runtime scheduling behavior, so this row closes as not-applicable rather than deferred.

manualEvidence.evidence: Source `gradle.properties` review confirms the file contains only daemon memory/JDK-open flags, AndroidX enablement, KAPT worker disabling, and Android resource/build toggles, with no scheduler declarations.
Target `EntryAbility.onCreate` review confirms phase-01 startup only resolves login state and loads bootstrap content, without any background-task replacement originating from this config file.

set.sourceBehavior: `gradle.properties` contains only build and daemon tuning flags and does not define intents, URI handlers, deeplinks, bundles, or cross-screen parameter wiring.
set.targetBehavior: HarmonyOS keeps phase-01 route flow in explicit route helpers rather than deriving any cross-screen parameter behavior from build config, so this source config row is not applicable.
verificationEvidence: manual-review: source `gradle.properties` has no URI scheme, navigation, or intent-related keys.
manual-review: target route registry and start-route helpers carry the phase-01 shell flow, independent of build config.
set.notes: This row was opened by config-file risk tagging, but the source file itself contains no deeplink or cross-screen argument contract.

manualEvidence.evidence: Source `gradle.properties` review confirms there are no intent, URI scheme, bundle, or deeplink declarations in the file.
Target route review confirms phase-01 screen flow is defined in `route_registry.cj` and `app_router.cj`, not in build config.

set.sourceBehavior: `gradle.properties` only contains build-system toggles such as `android.useAndroidX`, `kotlin.code.style`, and resource/build flags; it does not persist user preferences or define runtime option-toggle behavior.
set.targetBehavior: HarmonyOS keeps user session preferences in `LoginPreferencesService` and startup consumption in `StartupLaunchService`, while no runtime preference behavior is sourced from this Gradle config file.
verificationEvidence: manual-review: source `gradle.properties` contains developer/build toggles only, not user-facing preference state.
manual-review: target persisted preference behavior is implemented in `LoginPreferencesService` and consumed by `StartupLaunchService`, not in build config.
focused-test-pass: `phase02_startup_navigation_test.cj` covers `LoginPreferencesService.nextLoginState` and startup route resolution helpers.
set.notes: The source file has config toggles for the build toolchain, but not source-app preference behavior. Actual persisted login preference behavior is already tracked by the build.gradle/settings rows and target session services.

testEvidence.evidence: `entry/src/test/cangjie/phase02_startup_navigation_test.cj` covers the login-state toggle helper and startup route resolution helpers, but no accepted test-build report exists yet for strong harness evidence.

manualEvidence.evidence: Source `gradle.properties` review confirms the file contains developer/build toggles only and no user-facing preference persistence contract.
